title: Alsine rostrata
intro: Alsine rostrata, also known as Alsine or Sharp-leaved Alsine, is a perennial plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that grows in rocky, mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Alsine rostrata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Alsine rostrata is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, digestive problems, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Alsine rostrata is classified in the family Caryophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Alsine. Alsine rostrata is further categorized under the tribe Caryophylleae. This plant is often classified as a succulent. Alsine rostrata is a type of flowering plant.
